{{日本の氏族
|家名 = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko
|家紋 =
|家紋名称 =
|本姓 = {{ill|Kimikobe clan|ja|吉弥侯部|lt=Kimikobe clan|lt_ja=吉弥侯部氏}}
|家祖 = {{ill|Kaga-Wake|ja|鹿我別|lt=Kaga-Wake-no-Ōkimi|lt_ja=賀我別王}}
|種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}}
|出身地 = {{ill|Ōyamato Province|ja|大和国|lt=Ōyamato Province|lt_ja=大倭国}}
|根拠地 = Ukita Province<br />(later {{ill|Uda District, Mutsu|ja|宇多郡|lt=Uda District|lt_ja=宇多郡}}, {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|lt=Namekata District|lt_ja=行方郡}} of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}})
|人物 = See [[#People|People]]
|支流 = {{ill|Harima-Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}}<br />{{ill|Kamitsukenu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}}<br />{{ill|Shimotsukenu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}
}}
'''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''ukita no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''ukita kokuzō'') was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled [[#Territory|Ukita Province]] (eastern {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}).

== Overview ==
=== Ancestry ===
* According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|lt=Emperor Seimu|lt_ja=成務朝}}, '''{{ill|Kaga-Wake|ja|鹿我別|lt=Kaga-Wake-no-Ōkimi|lt_ja=賀我別王}}''', 5th-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, was appointed kuni no miyatsuko.

=== Clan ===
'''{{ill|Kimikobe clan|ja|吉弥侯部|lt=Kimikobe clan|lt_ja=吉弥侯部氏}}''' (''kimikobe-uji''; {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} unknown), kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukenu clan|ja|上毛野氏}}, {{ill|Shimotsukenu clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, and {{ill|Harima-Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造}}. On the [[July 19|19th day of the 7th month]] of {{ill|Jingo-keiun|ja|神護景雲}} 1 ([[767]]), Kimikobe-no-Ishimaro, a Ge-shō-rokui-jō, Kun-jūtō of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡}}, was granted the surname '''Kamitsukenu-Mutsu-{{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|キミ (カバネ)|lt=Kimi|lt_ja=公}}'''.

== Base ==
=== Territory ===
The territory of the kuni no miyatsuko was the region then called '''Ukita Province''', corresponding to the later {{ill|ritsuryō province|ja|律令国}} of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}} '''{{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡}}''' and '''{{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|lt=Namekata District|lt_ja=行方郡}}''', equivalent to present-day {{ill|Sōma, Fukushima|ja|相馬市|lt=Sōma City|lt_ja=相馬市}}, {{ill|Minamisōma, Fukushima|ja|南相馬市|lt=Minamisōma City|lt_ja=南相馬市}}, {{ill|Shinchi, Fukushima|ja|新地町|lt=Shinchi Town|lt_ja=新地町}} in {{ill|Sōma District, Fukushima|ja|相馬郡|lt=Sōma District|lt_ja=相馬郡}}, and {{ill|Iitate, Fukushima|ja|飯舘村|lt=Iitate Village|lt_ja=飯舘村}} in the same district. Contemporary with the establishment of the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko, the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} ({{ill|Igu District|ja|伊具郡}}) was placed to the northwest, and the {{ill|Somewa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} ({{ill|Shineha District|ja|標葉郡}}) to the south.

After the {{ill|Taika Reform|ja|大化の改新}}, when the {{ill|hyō system|ja|評|lt=hyō system|lt_ja=評制}} was implemented nationwide, there is a theory that the {{ill|Uda River|ja|宇多川}} basin became Uda-hyō, and the {{ill|Nitta River|ja|新田川}} basin became Namekata-hyō<ref>{{ill|Hirakawa Minami|ja|平川南}}, ''Tōhoku "Kaidō" no Kodaishi'', {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 2012, p. 122.</ref>.

== Tutelary Shrine ==
'''{{ill|Komi-ne Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社}}''', the sole {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡}} and a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}.

=== Tombs ===
*'''{{ill|Sakurai Kofun|ja|桜井古墳}}''' (''sakurai-kofun'')
*:A 74 m long {{ill|keyhole-rectangle kofun|ja|前方後方墳}} in {{ill|Minamisōma, Fukushima|ja|南相馬市|lt=Minamisōma City|lt_ja=南相馬市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, constructed in the late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} to the early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. Based on its location and construction date, there is a theory that it is the tomb of {{ill|Kaga-Wake-no-Mikoto|ja|鹿我別|lt=Kaga-Wake-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=鹿我別命}}, the first ancestor of the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm The Genealogy of the Madaramoku Clan of the Kimikobe Kabane]," ''Koju Kinobōma'', 2016.</ref>.
*'''{{ill|Mano Kofun Group|ja|真野古墳群}}''' (''mano-kofun-gun'')
*:A large-scale late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} in Minamisōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, composed of 2 {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} and numerous {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}}.

== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}

== See also ==
*{{ill|Fushū|ja|俘囚}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:うきたのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:陸奥国]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:浮田国造]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
